openQuestions.question: Does the evidence TSV reproducibly show runner:* SKIP with owning pids and a stable explanation?
Is the ~1437 graph-metadata.json churn pre-existing + operator background rescans, NOT harness-produced?
Is the SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true leak genuinely sidestepped by skip-not-fail, or merely hidden?

resolvedQuestions.question: Do genuine daemon crashes still FAIL via liveOwnerForService null path?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes. Null path at run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:364 catches genuine crashes when OS has not recycled the PID. Residual risk is PID recycling (F-005).

resolvedQuestions.question: Does the false-green guard still fire in a clean env?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes. Guard at substrate-runner-harness.vitest.ts:79-83 fires when no runner:mk-spec-memory SKIP row (daemons connected cleanly) and asserts 410 must be PASS/PARTIAL. If 410 SKIPs due to missing memory_search, the assertion fails. Only bypass is PID recycling F-005.

keyFindings.label: Null-return from liveOwnerForService is sole FAIL arbiter; genuine crash FAILs only when isPidAlive returns false for all lease pids
isPidAlive: ESRCH->false (dead), EPERM->true (alive), no-error->true (alive) — correctly implemented
DR-016 childPid not checked by harness: launcher crash with live child -> harness returns null -> FAIL (correct for harness purpose, but not a false-green)
TOCTOU window not exploitable: liveOwnerForService called synchronously in catch block with no await between client.close() and lease read
PID-recycling attack: if daemon crashes without cleaning lease AND OS recycles ownerPid to a live unrelated process, isPidAlive returns true and verdict becomes SKIP instead of FAIL — residual risk
runner: rows ONLY on connect/listTools throw; diagnostic:null only when both succeed without throwing; SKIP row means liveOwner!=null (daemon alive at PID level), not that connect succeeded

Guard catches all-SKIP false green: when no runner:mk-spec-memory SKIP row (both connect cleanly), guard asserts 410 must be PASS/PARTIAL; if 410 SKIPs (missing memory_search), assertion fails and test fails — guard is effective

Only bypass of guard is PID recycling F-005: dead daemon's lease not cleaned + ownerPid recycled to live unrelated process -> isPidAlive returns true -> liveOwner!=null -> SKIP row -> guard bypassed. No other mechanism.

listTools() failure is NOT independent false-green path: both connect-throw and listTools-throw enter same catch block and are classified by liveOwnerForService only; zombie daemon -> SKIP (correct, PID IS alive); dead daemon -> FAIL (correct)

410 guard is specifically about tool availability not harness infra: guards against 'daemon connects but silently exposes no memory_search tools' scenario; this is correct behavior for a clean-env validation

ruledOutDirections.direction: EPERM false-green independent of PID recycling
ruledOutDirections.reason: EPERM always means process is alive; EPERM cannot cause false-green except via the PID-recycling path (F-005) which requires a dead daemon's PID to be live-reassigned

ruledOutDirections.direction: TOCTOU between connect failure and lease read
ruledOutDirections.reason: liveOwnerForService is called synchronously in catch block with no await between client.close() and lease read; no meaningful race window
childPid not checked means harness FAILs when launcher is dead even if child is alive; not a false-green, but the harness purpose is launcher's ability to spawn a dedicated child

ruledOutDirections.direction: listTools zombie/unresponsive produces false-green SKIP without PID recycling
ruledOutDirections.reason: isPidAlive only checks if PID is alive (process exists), not if daemon is responsive; zombie daemon -> liveOwner!=null -> SKIP is correct (PID IS alive, harness cannot spawn child)

ruledOutDirections.direction: empty toolNames Set silently bypasses guard
ruledOutDirections.reason: empty toolNames -> memory_search absent -> 410 SKIPs -> guard fires -> assertion fails (not silent pass)

ruledOutDirections.direction: memoryScenario undefined (missing 410 row) silently bypasses guard
ruledOutDirections.reason: undefined verdict -> expect(['PASS','PARTIAL']).toContain(undefined) throws -> test fails, not silent pass
